title: Dan L'Kaf Zechut: Judging Others Favorably
title_zh: 有利判断他人:犹太智慧中的
title_he: דן לכף זכות: לשפוט אחרים לטובה

sources.description: Core teaching by Yehoshua ben Perachya on judging favorably
Story of the laborer and employer demonstrating dan l'kaf zechut; teaching that those who judge favorably are themselves judged favorably
Maimonides' explanation of when and how to judge favorably

sources.text: Chofetz Chaim (Rabbi Yisrael Meir Kagan) on Shmirat HaLashon
sources.description: Guidance on balancing favorable judgment with communal protection
己所不欲,勿施于人 - The Golden Rule creating space for understanding others
Empty boat parable - anger depends on assumptions about intention
恕道 - The way of forbearance and putting oneself in others' positions

key_concepts: Chinese parallel: 恕道 (way of forbearance)
target_audience: Chinese readers new to Judaism seeking practical ethical guidance with cultural bridges
chinese_cultural_bridges: 给面子 (gěi miànzi) - giving face as parallel to favorable judgment
Confucian Golden Rule: 己所不欲,勿施于人
Zhuangzi's empty boat teaching about assumptions and anger
Zhu Xi's 恕道 (way of forbearance)
吕坤's teaching on treating others with leniency
practical_applications: When someone doesn't respond to messages
author: Torah Light Project
